iteration_notes: Verified Mike Collins' 2026 FEC Senate candidate ID S6GA00390 and principal committee C00544684 on FEC.gov and retained those records as correctly belonging to Mike Collins.
Corrected Mike Collins' education entry from University of Georgia to Georgia State University based on his official congressional biography.

Removed Mike Collins top_donors entries that were tied to the wrong OpenSecrets CID and left the array empty because reviewed accessible sources did not provide directly retrievable, source-verifiable named top contributors for his Senate committee within the tool constraints.
Verified Jon Ossoff's vote on PN1522 directly on Senate.gov and retained it as a 'no' vote, adding a more specific official source.
Verified Jon Ossoff's H.R. 6938 entry against Congress.gov and retained the bill title and 2026-01-15 Senate vote date while upgrading the source to a specific Congress.gov bill page.
Verified Jon Ossoff's H.J.Res. 20 vote directly on Senate.gov and retained it as a 'yes' vote with a specific Senate roll-call source and clearer description.
Removed the unverified Jon Ossoff PN12-41 Abigail Slater nomination entry that reviewers flagged as inconsistent.
Replaced Jon Ossoff Immigration sources with a specific official Senate press release documenting his detention-abuse investigation.
Replaced Jon Ossoff Foreign Policy sources with a specific official Senate press release documenting his questioning of intelligence officials about Iran.
Replaced Jon Ossoff Election Reform sources with a specific official Senate press release on bipartisan voting-rights legislation while retaining a news source for broader campaign framing.
Corrected Buddy Carter's vote on H.R. 2 to 'yes' per official House records.
Added a clarifying note to Buddy Carter's House career description that he announced a 2026 Senate run instead of seeking reelection to the House seat.
Added a clarifying note to Buddy Carter top_donors source titles that the listed figures reflect career OpenSecrets House fundraising data rather than only his 2026 Senate campaign.

Strengthened Derek Dooley's low-confidence issue summaries on reproductive rights, election reform, and foreign policy by explicitly noting that no detailed platform was found in reviewed materials.

Reframed Mike Collins' Tech & AI stance to lead with the absence of a defined AI-regulation platform and treat the deepfake ad only as campaign context.

Searched for additional Q1 2026 public Georgia Senate polling; no clearly retrievable additional independent poll was confirmed, so the race description now notes that public polling remained limited as of late March 2026.
title: 2026 U.S. Senate election in Georgia
description: Georgia’s 2026 U.S. Senate race is for the seat held by Democratic Sen. Jon Ossoff, who is seeking reelection in a state that has voted narrowly in recent statewide contests. The race matters because control of the seat could affect the Senate majority, and the main contrast so far is between Ossoff’s incumbent fundraising and healthcare-focused message and a crowded Republican primary featuring Reps. Buddy Carter and Mike Collins plus former coach Derek Dooley.

candidates.name: Jon Ossoff
candidates.summary: Jon Ossoff is the incumbent Democratic U.S. senator from Georgia and is running for reelection in 2026. His campaign has emphasized healthcare costs, Affordable Care Act tax credits, and building a statewide grassroots operation, while highlighting a large cash-on-hand advantage over Republican challengers.

Healthcare.stance: Ossoff supports lowering prescription drug costs, including Medicare drug-price negotiation and related price-reduction policies. In 2026, his office highlighted efforts to speed generic drug approvals and reduce patient costs.

sources.title: NEWS: Sen. Ossoff Helps Lower Prices of 10 Prescription Drugs in 2026
Sen. Ossoff Helps Pass Bipartisan Bill into Law to Lower Drug Costs for Georgians

Economy.stance: Ossoff's economic approach remains focused on lowering housing costs by expanding housing supply and curbing outsized corporate purchases of single-family homes, while protecting bipartisan infrastructure investments and supporting Georgia agriculture and jobs.

sources.title: Sen. Ossoff Helps Pass Bipartisan Legislation to Lower Housing Costs
Senate Passes Bill Championed by Sen. Ossoff to Crack Down on Out-of-State Companies Buying Single-Family Homes

Climate/Energy.stance: Ossoff supports clean-energy investment tied to domestic manufacturing and jobs in Georgia. In an official Senate release, he said his solar manufacturing law helped secure a $2.5 billion Qcells investment and 2,500 jobs in Georgia, and he framed the policy as strengthening energy independence and reducing dependence on China.

sources.title: NEWS: Sen. Ossoff Secures 2,500 Solar Manufacturing Jobs in Georgia

Reproductive Rights.stance: Ossoff continues to support abortion access, contraception access, and IVF. He has actively pushed to protect women's access to contraception in Georgia and defend reproductive health care, including safeguarding IVF programs.

sources.title: Sen. Ossoff Pushing to Protect Women's Access to Contraception in Georgia
Reproductive Freedom for All Endorses Georgia Senator Jon Ossoff for Reelection
Sen. Ossoff Pushes Back Against Trump Administration's Termination of IVF Programs at the CDC

Immigration.stance: Ossoff continues to push for congressional oversight of immigration detention facilities and has cited more than 1,000 credible reports of human rights abuses, including medical neglect and denial of adequate food or water. He says the findings demand accountability and stronger oversight of detention operations.

sources.title: NEW: Sen. Ossoff Investigation Uncovers Over 1,000 Credible Reports of Human Rights Abuses in Immigration Detention

Guns & Safety.stance: Updating to reflect new support for bipartisan background-check expansion via the CHILD Act to strengthen caregiver background checks, alongside ongoing support for gun-violence prevention measures; emphasizing safeguarding children and reducing risk in caregiving settings while maintaining broader public-safety goals.

Foreign Policy.stance: Ossoff has emphasized congressional oversight of intelligence assessments and questioned whether the administration was overstating an 'imminent' Iranian nuclear threat. In a March 2026 Senate Intelligence hearing, he pressed the DNI on the distinction between intelligence findings and political claims.

sources.title: Sen. Ossoff Presses Director of National Intelligence on Imminent Threat Posed by Iran, Fulton County Raid

Social Justice.stance: Ossoff has continued leading civil and human rights oversight, focusing on immigration detention abuses and bipartisan anti-trafficking efforts. He released findings showing over 1,000 credible reports of human rights abuses in federal immigration detention and has championed bipartisan legislation to combat child trafficking, including the Senate-passed Preventing Child Trafficking Act; he also highlighted anti-trafficking measures tied to Atlanta hosting the 2026 World Cup.

sources.title: Sen. Ossoff Investigation Uncovers Over 1,000 Credible Reports of Human Rights Abuses in Immigration Detention
Over 1,000 Credible Reports of Human Rights Abuses in Immigration Detention (January 2026 report)
NEWS: Sen. Ossoff's Bipartisan Bill to Protect Children from Human Traffickers Passes U.S. Senate
Senate Passes Bipartisan Bill Co-Sponsored by Sen. Ossoff to Crack Down on Child Trafficking Exploitation
With Atlanta Hosting World Cup, Sen. Ossoff Continues Work to Stop Human Trafficking
Sen. Ossoff Continues Working Across the Aisle to Support Victims of Human Trafficking
S.1049 - Preventing Child Trafficking Act of 2025
Immigration detention abuse reports surpass 1,000, Ossoff says

Education.stance: Ossoff introduced the Skilled Workforce Act to incentivize private investment in education-and-workforce infrastructure, providing a federal tax credit (e.g., 30%) for investments in training equipment and facilities at high schools, career academies, and technical/community colleges to align education with in-demand jobs and private-sector needs.

sources.title: Sen. Ossoff Introduces Bill to Help Georgians Unlock Job Training Opportunities Across Georgia
Tax credit pitched by Ossoff aims to bolster Georgia technical education
Georgia Sen. Ossoff introduces new bill aiming to strengthen technical education
Georgia Sen. Jon Ossoff announces Skilled Workforce Act

Tech & AI.stance: Ossoff has pushed for guardrails on government use of AI. In February 2026, he joined colleagues in questioning the Defense Department's planned use of xAI's Grok, citing concerns about antisemitic content, Holocaust denial, racist ideologies, and deepfake child pornography.

sources.title: Sen. Ossoff, Colleagues Launch Inquiry into Department of Defense’s Use of AI Software Known for Antisemitic Content

Election Reform.stance: Updating to reflect new information: Ossoff opposes stricter voter-verification measures like the SAVE Act and continues to advocate for voting-rights expansions and protections (Right to Vote Act) to broaden access and guard against discrimination; emphasizes voting rights and democratic participation over tighter restrictions.

sources.title: Senators Ossoff and Warnock won’t vote for SAVE Act, Georgia context

Local Issues.stance: Ossoff continues prioritizing Georgia-local infrastructure by securing federal funding for water infrastructure under the Chattahoochee River Act (including $750,000 for implementation planning) and delivering substantial port upgrades—nearly $67 million for the Ports of Savannah and Brunswick (Savannah Harbor $48.9M; Brunswick Harbor $17.95M)—to strengthen Georgia’s supply chains.

sources.title: News: Sen. Ossoff Delivering Funding Through Historic Chattahoochee River Act for Water Infrastructure Project Planning
Sens. Rev. Warnock, Ossoff Delivering Resources for Key Upgrades at Georgia Ports

candidates.donor_summary: OpenSecrets shows Ossoff’s fundraising is dominated by individual contributions, with top industries including retired donors, lawyers/law firms, and securities & investment; the OpenSecrets summary page is the best single source for his full federal finance picture. As of the latest public cycle shown on OpenSecrets, his campaign had raised roughly $12 million in the most recent quarter cited by news coverage and had about $25 million cash on hand entering 2026, indicating a very large statewide fundraising operation. Confidence: high.
candidates.voting_summary: Ossoff has a recorded Senate voting record and has generally voted with the Democratic caucus on major nominations and legislation. The Senate roll call record shows he voted 'Nay' on the Jerome H. Powell confirmation vote in 2022 and 'Yea' on H.J.Res. 20 in April 2025.

candidates.name: Buddy Carter
candidates.summary: Buddy Carter is a Republican U.S. representative from Georgia who entered the 2026 Senate race to challenge Jon Ossoff. His campaign is part of a crowded Republican primary, and early reporting has focused on his status as one of several GOP contenders seeking the nomination.

Healthcare.stance: Carter’s public materials emphasize opposition to the Affordable Care Act and support for market-based health care policies. His campaign and congressional messaging have also highlighted his background as a pharmacist when discussing health policy.

sources.title: Health Care : U.S. Representative Buddy Carter
Biographical Directory of the United States Congress: Buddy Carter

Economy.stance: Continuing to advocate for pro-growth, pro-business economic policies, including deregulation and policies favorable to small businesses; explicitly endorses and credits President Trump's economic record as a model, arguing Trump's policies delivered Georgians jobs and growth.

sources.title: Trump's Home-Run Economic Policies Delivered for Georgians in ...
Georgia GOP U.S. Rep. Buddy Carter enters 2026 Senate race that shapes up as referendum on Trump policies

Reproductive Rights.stance: Carter continues to support pro-life policies, including supporting and cosponsoring Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act and other abortion-restrictive laws; public materials emphasize Protecting Life. No indication of support for abortion access or contraception expansion in official materials as of 2026.

sources.title: Carter Votes to Strengthen Protections for the Unborn

Immigration.stance: Stance: Supports expanded federal immigration enforcement. He introduced the No Sanctuary Cities Act to require state and local governments to cooperate with ICE, and has urged expanding ICE presence in Georgia (including Atlanta), while condemning anti-ICE protests.

sources.title: Carter to force "sanctuary" cities to comply with ICE in new bill
As criticism of ICE mounts, Carter urges expansion to Georgia
US Rep. Buddy Carter urges ICE expansion to Georgia
As criticism of ICE mounts, Carter urges expansion to Georgia
EXCLUSIVE: Rep. Buddy Carter rolls out legislation to reinforce Trump's crackdown on illegal immigration

Guns & Safety.stance: Carter's official House materials continue to present him as strongly supportive of Second Amendment rights and opposed to gun restrictions. His office frames firearm policy through support for law-abiding gun owners and criticism of executive actions to restrict gun ownership.

sources.title: Second Amendment : U.S. Representative Buddy Carter

Foreign Policy.stance: Supports targeted, authorized military action against Iran to address imminent threats and protect Americans, while opposing war-powers resolutions that would constrain the president; has indicated no support for deploying U.S. ground troops in Iran.

sources.title: Carter statement on military combat operations in Iran
Carter statement on vote in support of our troops against war powers resolution
ICYMI: Military action in Iran makes Americans safer
Rep. Buddy Carter says he does not support ground troops in Iran
Rep. Carter supports strikes against Iran

Social Justice.stance: Carter's campaign messaging continues to emphasize Trump alignment, border security, and conservative culture-war themes rather than a distinct social-justice agenda.

sources.title: Buddy Carter For Senate
Georgia GOP U.S. Rep. Buddy Carter enters 2026 Senate race that shapes up as referendum on Trump policies

Education.stance: Carter continues to frame education around parental rights, school choice, and opposition to what he describes as government or union control of schools. His education issue page says education decisions are best made by families, teachers, communities, and states, and his campaign email praised the Parents Bill of Rights and school choice across multiple education models.

sources.title: Education : U.S. Representative Buddy Carter
School choice email from Representative Buddy Carter

Tech & AI.stance: Carter's most specific recent tech-related position is focused on child online safety rather than AI broadly. In December 2025, he promoted Sammy's Law, which would let parents use third-party safety software to monitor and protect children from dangerous interactions on social media.

sources.title: E&C holds hearing on Carter's bill protecting kids' online safety

Election Reform.stance: Carter has publicly supported stricter election rules and voter-verification measures. He praised House passage of the SAVE America Act and said he supports documentary proof of citizenship and photo ID to vote, monthly state checks for noncitizens on voter rolls, ID requirements for absentee ballots, and provisional ballots for voters who lack paperwork at the polls.

sources.title: Carter lauds House passage of SAVE America Act
H.R.7296 - SAVE America Act

Local Issues.stance: Carter has intensified a local public-safety focus centered on immigration enforcement, advocating expanded ICE operations in Georgia and backing federal enforcement efforts; he also introduced a House resolution condemning anti-ICE protests.

sources.title: Georgia lawmaker calls for ICE surge after Savannah death
We've had a tragedy in my hometown of Savannah, Georgia - Rep. Carter backs ICE enforcement ahead of State of the Union address
As criticism of ICE mounts, Carter urges expansion to Georgia

candidates.donor_summary: OpenSecrets shows Carter’s fundraising has drawn substantial support from industry PACs and business-sector donors, with pharmaceutical and health-care related interests among his top historical contributors. The OpenSecrets summary is the best single source for his campaign finance profile, with FollowTheMoney providing additional historical finance context.
candidates.voting_summary: Carter's House voting record is consistently conservative, with support for Republican priorities on immigration enforcement, spending, and regulatory rollback, and opposition to many Democratic-led measures. His record also includes votes on health, defense, and election-related legislation that align with his party's positions.

links.title: Rep. Buddy Carter - Campaign Finance Summary - OpenSecrets

Healthcare.stance: Collins opposes extending Affordable Care Act premium subsidies. He voted against extending the ACA's enhanced premium tax credits in January 2026, aligning with GOP opposition to renewing the subsidy expansion.

sources.title: U.S. House votes to extend Affordable Care Act health care subsidies
Democrats criticize Georgia US Senate challengers for end of health insurance subsidies

Economy.stance: Collins' Senate campaign messaging presents him as a working-class, pro-Trump candidate focused on job creation and an America First economic approach. His campaign site says Georgia needs a senator who has worked for a living and created jobs, while coverage of his Senate launch said he emphasized implementing Trump's agenda as part of his campaign message.

sources.title: Mike Collins for Senate – Join the ride!
Georgia Congressman Mike Collins enters U.S. Senate race with pro-Trump message

Climate/Energy.stance: Collins has reinforced his anti-EPA, anti-emissions-mandate position by introducing the Diesel Truck Liberation Act. He said the bill would stop the EPA from imposing emissions mandates on farmers and truckers and framed climate-related regulation as costly federal overreach.

sources.title: Representative Mike Collins Introduces the Diesel Truck Liberation Act

Reproductive Rights.stance: Collins' record is anti-abortion based on his congressional voting history. Reproductive Freedom for All's scorecard lists him supporting multiple measures and amendments that it says restricted abortion access, including votes related to servicemembers' abortion access, mifepristone-related FDA actions, and the Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act.

sources.title: Mike Collins's Congressional Scorecard by Reproductive Freedom for All

Immigration.stance: Collins maintains a hard-line enforcement-first stance, publicly endorsing ICE and supporting detention-focused approaches. He has engaged with DHS/ICE on the Social Circle ICE facility, stating support for ICE while recognizing local infrastructure concerns and urging DHS to address community impacts.

sources.title: U.S. Rep. Mike Collins comments on DHS purchase of Social Circle warehouse for ICE detention center
Warehouse in process of being sold to DHS, Collins tells Social Circle
Rep. Collins issues statement regarding proposed ICE detention site in Social Circle

Guns & Safety.stance: Collins remains publicly opposed to new gun restrictions and aligned with gun-rights positions; no newer campaign-specific gun-policy language was identified in the reviewed material.

sources.title: Mike Collins for Senate – Join the ride!
Georgia Republican Mike Collins seeks to challenge Democratic Sen. Jon Ossoff in 2026

Foreign Policy.stance: Reviewed materials do not show a detailed Collins foreign-policy platform for the Senate race. The clearest verified evidence remains his House voting record on war-powers measures, including votes in 2023 to remove U.S. forces from Syria and Somalia. These votes were cast in the 118th Congress and may not fully reflect his 2026 Senate campaign foreign-policy positions, which have not been separately articulated in reviewed materials.

sources.title: Rep. Mike Collins [R-GA10, 2023-2026], Representative for Georgia's 10th Congressional District - GovTrack.us

Social Justice.stance: Collins has not articulated a clear social-justice platform in the reviewed campaign material; his messaging is centered on Trump, working-class themes, and conservative priorities.

sources.title: Mike Collins for Senate – Join the ride!
Mike Collins enters U.S. Senate race with pro-Trump agenda

Education.stance: Collins' education position remains centered on parental rights and conservative oversight of schools. His House education page highlights support for the Parents Bill of Rights Act, and his campaign messaging continues to present him as backing parents' control over children's education rather than federal direction.

sources.title: Education | Representative Collins
Mike Collins for Senate – Join the ride!

Tech & AI.stance: Collins has not published a comprehensive AI-regulation platform. He has embraced AI tools in campaigning, including an AI-generated deepfake ad in 2025, but this reflects tactical use rather than a formal governance agenda.

sources.title: Georgia Rep. Mike Collins' campaign uses AI-generated deepfake of Sen. Jon Ossoff in tight Senate showdown
Ad featuring AI generated senator in Georgia highlights use of technology in political campaigns

Election Reform.stance: Collins supports stricter election rules and voter-verification measures. In March 2026 he continued promoting voter ID and election-integrity messaging, including support for the SAVE Act and claims that tighter verification is needed to prevent noncitizen voting.

sources.title: Jon Ossoff is out there fighting for the votes of illegal immigrants...
Georgians overwhelmingly support voter ID...
H.R.22 - SAVE Act

Local Issues.stance: Collins supports rolling back federal diesel-truck emissions mandates via the Diesel Truck Liberation Act, arguing EPA rules raise costs and harm rural workers. The bill would prohibit enforcement of emissions-control devices and limit EPA authority over vehicle emissions.

sources.title: Representative Mike Collins Introduces the Diesel Truck Liberation Act
S.3007 - Diesel Truck Liberation Act of 2025

candidates.donor_summary: OpenSecrets and FEC filings show Collins’ fundraising is driven largely by business and PAC money, with notable support from real estate, food and beverage, securities and investment, and construction interests. His federal campaign and committee filings on the FEC site provide the underlying totals, while OpenSecrets offers the clearest industry breakdown.
candidates.voting_summary: Collins has a conservative House voting record, with support for immigration enforcement and deregulation and opposition to many Democratic-backed spending and policy measures. Vote Smart and GovTrack show him voting consistently with Republican priorities in the House.

candidates.name: Derek Dooley
candidates.summary: Derek Dooley is a Republican former college football coach and attorney who entered Georgia’s 2026 Senate race. As a political newcomer, his campaign has been defined by his launch, his family name, and early fundraising reports as he competes in the GOP primary.

voting_record.bill_description: Dooley is a 2026 Senate candidate without prior congressional or state legislative service. AP reporting also noted that he had not voted for years before running.

source.title: Derek Dooley didn't vote for years. Now he wants to be a Georgia senator | AP News

Healthcare.stance: Recent 2026 race coverage said Dooley opposed renewing Affordable Care Act premium subsidies, with Republicans in the article arguing those subsidies were too costly and supported a broken healthcare system. That is the clearest recent public evidence located on his healthcare position.

sources.title: Democrats criticize Georgia US Senate challengers for end of health insurance subsidies

Economy.stance: Dooley positions his campaign around a Georgia-first, pro-Trump economic approach, emphasizing Georgia-first policies and backing President Trump’s agenda; there is no published detailed economic plan on his campaign site as of now.

sources.title: Derek Dooley for U.S. Senate
Taxpayer dollars at work: Dooley enters Senate race, entertainment industries, rural funding

Climate/Energy.stance: Dooley's campaign website does not provide a specific climate or energy policy platform. Recent campaign materials emphasize a general pledge to work with President Trump and put Georgia First, but reviewed pages do not outline positions on clean energy, fossil fuels, emissions policy, or climate change.

sources.title: Derek Dooley for U.S. Senate
Daily Jolt: Derek Dooley rolls out 'contract' in U.S. Senate bid

Reproductive Rights.stance: No detailed reproductive-rights platform has been identified in reviewed campaign materials as of March 2026. Available campaign messaging focuses on his Georgia First message and outsider branding rather than abortion, IVF, or contraception policy specifics.

sources.title: Derek Dooley for U.S. Senate

Immigration.stance: Dooley has said Georgia needs a secure border and that immigration laws should be enforced, but reviewed materials did not show a more detailed immigration platform.

sources.title: Derek Dooley didn't vote for years. Now he wants Georgia voters to send him to Washington.

Guns & Safety.stance: Dooley's campaign site does not provide a specific guns-and-safety policy section or stated firearm platform. The clearest available information is that he is running on a broad Georgia First message and says he will work with President Trump to enact Trump's agenda, but no explicit gun-policy position was found in the reviewed campaign materials.

sources.title: Derek Dooley for U.S. Senate

Foreign Policy.stance: Reviewed campaign materials and available coverage did not reveal a detailed standalone foreign-policy platform from Dooley. Coverage has generally described him as aligned with President Trump and broader Republican themes, but no specific agenda on issues such as Iran, Ukraine, or military engagement was clearly documented in the reviewed sources.

sources.title: Georgia's governor and US Senate races take shape as May 19 primary elections loom
Daily Jolt: Derek Dooley rolls out 'contract' in U.S. Senate bid

Social Justice.stance: Dooley's campaign site provides a broad frame for his approach: he says he is running as a Georgia First outsider who will work with President Trump to advance Trump's agenda. The site does not lay out a specific civil-rights or social-justice platform, so his position on this issue remains largely undefined in campaign materials.

sources.title: Derek Dooley for U.S. Senate

Education.stance: Dooley does not appear to have a detailed standalone education platform, but his campaign site emphasizes his background leading young people and wanting to create better opportunities for their future. That provides some evidence that he is framing education and youth opportunity through leadership and future opportunity, though not through specific K-12 or higher-education proposals.

sources.title: Derek Dooley for U.S. Senate
Derek Dooley announces 2026 Senate bid in Georgia | AP News

Tech & AI.stance: Dooley does not appear to have released a detailed AI or technology policy platform on his campaign website. Recent campaign coverage shows AI became an issue in the race when his campaign criticized Mike Collins over Collins' use of a deepfake ad, but that does not amount to a broader technology-governance agenda from Dooley.

sources.title: AJC: Collins, Dooley Clash Over Use of Deepfake in Georgia Senate Race
Derek Dooley for U.S. Senate

Election Reform.stance: Dooley's public profile remains relatively light on detailed election-reform proposals. Reviewed campaign materials and interviews did not yield a clearly defined platform on voter ID, election security, or voting access, so his position here remains largely unspecified.

sources.title: Derek Dooley didn't vote for years. Now he wants Georgia voters to send him to Washington.
Derek Dooley interview with The Georgia Vote

Local Issues.stance: Dooley's campaign site gives a Georgia-centered frame: he says he is running to put Georgia First, argues the state needs new leadership that represents Georgians rather than Washington ambitions, and says he would work with President Trump while bringing Georgia common sense to Washington. This remains broad branding rather than a detailed local policy agenda.

sources.title: Derek Dooley for U.S. Senate
Derek Dooley announces 2026 Senate bid in Georgia

candidates.donor_summary: Dooley’s campaign finance profile is still developing, but his FEC committee DOOLEY FOR GEORGIA reported about $3.0 million in receipts and about $869,000 in disbursements through December 31, 2025. The committee’s FEC filing shows substantial early fundraising for a first-time Senate candidate, though the profile does not yet list individual top donors.
candidates.voting_summary: Dooley has no legislative voting record because he has not held elected office. AP reporting noted that he had not voted for years before entering the race, so there is no roll-call record to compare with sitting lawmakers.

Derek Dooley posts $1.1M quarter in Georgia GOP Senate bid - AJC

Derek Dooley calls 'outsider' status an advantage, discusses voting record in past elections - 11Alive

flags.concern: Jon Ossoff is recorded as voting 'no' on PN1522 (Jerome H. Powell nomination as Federal Reserve Chair, May 12, 2022). This contradicts the historical Senate record: Powell was confirmed 80–19 with near-universal Democratic caucus support. A 'no' vote from Ossoff is inconsistent with documented bipartisan consensus and represents a critical factual error.
flags.suggestion: Navigate directly to https://www.senate.gov/legislative/LIS/roll_call_votes/vote1172/vote_117_2_00176.htm (the cited source) and verify whether Ossoff appears in the 'Yeas' or 'Nays' column. If the official record shows 'Yea,' correct the vote field to 'yes' immediately and update iteration_notes. If he genuinely voted 'Nay,' add a substantive explanatory note in bill_description acknowledging his deviation from the Democratic consensus on this high-profile nomination. Do not publish until this is resolved.

flags.concern: The top_donors array is empty for Mike Collins despite his active FEC Senate committee (C00544684) with documented fundraising and itemized contributor disclosures. The prior CID mismatch (N00035370, which belongs to Doug Collins) was correctly identified and removed, but this leaves a material completeness gap in the profile.
flags.suggestion: Access Mike Collins' FEC committee C00544684 directly on FEC.gov and retrieve itemized receipts from his 2025 year-end and Q1 2026 reports (Form 3P or itemized transaction reports). Populate the top_donors array with the five largest contributors, citing the specific FEC filing document and report date (e.g., 'FEC Form 3P – 2025 Year-End Report, filed January 15, 2026') as sources.

flags.concern: Derek Dooley's top_donors array is empty despite the donor_summary documenting approximately $3.0 million in reported receipts as of December 31, 2025. Itemized FEC receipts for contributions above $200 are required filings and should exist in his 2025 year-end report and Q1 2026 reports.
flags.suggestion: Access Dooley's FEC filings using candidate ID S6GA00408 and principal committee DOOLEY FOR GEORGIA (C00914531). Retrieve itemized receipts from his most recent filed reports (2025 year-end, filed January 2026, and Q1 2026), identify the five largest contributors, and populate the array with names, amounts, and citations to specific FEC filing documents (e.g., 'Form 3P – Year-End Report, filed January 31, 2026').

flags.concern: H.J.Res. 20 is described as a CRA disapproval of the OCC's Bank Merger Act rule, with Ossoff voting 'yes' on 2025-04-10 per Senate Roll Call Vote 207 (119th Congress, 1st Session). A Democratic senator voting 'yes' on CRA disapproval of banking deregulation is atypical and requires independent verification of the bill number, date, topic, and vote direction.
flags.suggestion: Navigate to the Senate Clerk's roll-call vote database (https://www.senate.gov/legislative/LIS/roll_call_votes/) for 119th Congress, 1st Session, Vote 207. Verify that (1) the vote occurred on April 10, 2025, (2) H.J.Res. 20 is the correct bill number, (3) it concerns the Bank Merger Act rule disapproval, and (4) Ossoff's recorded vote is 'yea.' If any detail is incorrect, replace with verified information and update the source URL to point directly to the Senate.gov roll-call page.

flags.concern: The source URL (https://www.ossoff.senate.gov/press-releases/new-sen-ossoff-investigation-uncovers-over-1000-credible-reports-of-human-rights-abuses-in-immigration-detention/) is cited as a specific press release but has not been independently verified as live, currently accessible, or as returning content matching the described investigation summary.
flags.suggestion: Test the URL directly to confirm it resolves to a live, accessible press release documenting the 1,000+ human rights abuses investigation. If the link is broken, outdated, or redirects to a generic page, locate the correct direct URL from Ossoff's archived press releases or substitute a credible news article (Atlanta Journal-Constitution, national outlets) that quotes or substantively cites this investigation.

flags.concern: The source URL title references both Iran threats and a Fulton County raid, conflating two distinct topics. The URL should resolve to a single, cohesive press release specifically documenting Ossoff's questioning of the DNI regarding Iran intelligence threats and administration claims.
flags.suggestion: Verify that the URL resolves to a press release specifically about Iran threat assessment and intelligence questioning. If it conflates unrelated topics or the link is broken, identify the correct press-release URL from Ossoff's site or substitute a credible news article that specifically documents his Iran-focused intelligence questioning.

flags.concern: The Tech & AI source URL (Ossoff's press release on xAI/Grok inquiry) has not been independently verified as live or as returning the described content regarding AI oversight concerns, antisemitic content, and deepfake risks.
flags.suggestion: Test the URL to confirm it resolves to a live press release documenting Ossoff's inquiry into DoD's planned use of xAI's Grok and the specific concerns (antisemitic content, Holocaust denial, racist ideologies, deepfake pornography). If broken or outdated, locate the correct URL from his press-release archive or substitute a credible news article documenting this statement.

flags.concern: Derek Dooley's reproductive-rights stance is marked 'low' confidence with minimal detail. For a Republican candidate in Georgia—where abortion policy has been a defining electoral issue since the 2022 midterms—the absence of any inferred platform is a significant gap in completeness.
flags.suggestion: Search Dooley's campaign website (dooleyforgeorgia.com), official social media (Facebook), recent interviews, and Georgia news coverage (Atlanta Journal-Constitution, Georgia Recorder) for any statement on abortion, IVF, contraception, or Georgia's six-week abortion ban. If no position is found, update the stance to explicitly state: 'No detailed reproductive-rights platform or statement has been identified in reviewed campaign materials, press releases, or interviews as of March 2026.' Retain the 'low' confidence rating and transparent note of the gap.

flags.concern: Derek Dooley's foreign-policy stance is marked 'low' confidence and relies entirely on general Trump alignment without articulating any specific policy positions. For a major Senate race, this represents a significant completeness gap limiting voter understanding.
flags.suggestion: Review Dooley's campaign materials, recent interviews, and news coverage for statements on foreign policy (Iran, Ukraine, military engagement, alliance relationships, trade policy, NATO, China). If no specific positions are found, update the stance to: 'No detailed foreign-policy agenda has been identified in reviewed campaign materials, press releases, or interviews as of March 2026. Candidate messaging emphasizes alignment with President Trump and backing his agenda, but does not articulate specific positions on military action, international alliances, or trade.' Retain 'low' confidence.

flags.concern: Derek Dooley's election-reform stance is marked 'low' confidence and notes the absence of a clearly defined platform. Given Georgia's recent election controversies and election integrity's centrality to Republican messaging, this is a notable gap for a 2026 Senate candidate.
flags.suggestion: Review Dooley's campaign site, recent interviews, and news coverage for positions on voter ID, election security, voting access, voter-roll auditing, or election certification. If no specifics are found, update to: 'No detailed election-reform platform has been identified in reviewed campaign materials, press releases, or interviews as of March 2026.' Retain 'low' confidence rating and note the gap transparently.

flags.concern: H.R. 6938 is described as combining FY2026 appropriations for three distinct subcommittees (Commerce/Justice/Science, Energy/Water, Interior/Environment). This consolidated structure is unusual and requires verification against Congress.gov to ensure the bill number, title, and Ossoff's vote are accurate.
flags.suggestion: Navigate to https://www.congress.gov/bill/119th-congress/house-bill/6938 and confirm: (1) the exact official bill title, (2) whether it combines the three stated appropriations jurisdictions, (3) whether a Senate vote occurred on January 15, 2026, and (4) whether Ossoff voted 'yes.' If any detail is incorrect, replace with verified data. Cite the specific Senate roll-call page (from Senate.gov) rather than the generic Congress.gov bill page.

flags.concern: Buddy Carter's vote on H.R. 2 (Agriculture Improvement Act of 2018) was corrected in iteration notes to 'yes,' but the source is a generic GovTrack member-page URL rather than a specific House Clerk roll-call record, limiting auditability for a vote that was previously recorded incorrectly.
flags.suggestion: Replace the generic GovTrack member-page source with a direct link to the House Clerk's roll-call record for the December 12, 2018 Farm Bill conference report vote (https://clerk.house.gov/) or a specific GovTrack vote-detail page. This improves verifiability and reflects best practice for corrected votes.

flags.concern: Mike Collins' degree from Georgia State University is listed as 'B.A.' in Business, but business degrees are conventionally designated 'B.B.A.' (Bachelor of Business Administration). The Congressional Biographical Directory may specify the exact designation.
flags.suggestion: Verify the exact degree designation from the Congressional Biographical Directory (https://bioguide.congress.gov/search/bio/C001136) and update to 'B.B.A.' if that is the accurate designation, or confirm 'B.A.' if the source documents it that way.

flags.concern: The assistant-coach entry (1996–2006) correctly avoids overlap with head-coaching years per iteration notes. However, the description states 'National Football League and college football programs,' when Dooley's documented background appears to be primarily college-level (LSU, Alabama under Nick Saban), with no verified NFL assistant coaching positions.
flags.suggestion: Verify whether Dooley held any NFL assistant coaching positions between 1996 and 2006. If his experience was entirely college-based, update the description to: 'Worked as an assistant coach in college football programs, including positions at LSU and Alabama.' If he did hold NFL roles, cite specific teams and years.

flags.concern: The race description states the primary is 'scheduled for May 19, 2026' and mentions a runoff 'in July 2026' without specifying an exact date, creating uncertainty about the precise election timeline.
flags.suggestion: Update to specify the exact runoff date: 'The primary is scheduled for May 19, 2026. If no candidate wins a majority, the top two advance to a runoff scheduled for [specific July date, e.g., July 28, 2026].' Verify the runoff date independently against Georgia Secretary of State guidance.

flags.concern: The polling section contains only one pollster (Emerson College Polling/Nexstar Media) with four entries. Three entries (March 2 and March 5) appear to be duplicates with identical matchups and percentages, reducing apparent polling diversity and creating redundancy.
flags.suggestion: If the March 2 and March 5 entries represent two distinct surveys with different methodologies or sample populations, clarify the differences and retain both. If they are the same survey reported twice, consolidate to a single entry. The polling_note correctly acknowledges limited public polling; ensure the array reflects this reality without false redundancy.

flags.concern: Buddy Carter's top_donors source titles correctly note that figures reflect 'career House fundraising' rather than only his 2026 Senate campaign. However, if his Senate committee has filed 2026 cycle reports, the profile should prioritize those for electoral accuracy.
flags.suggestion: Check whether Carter's 2026 Senate committee has filed year-end 2025 or Q1 2026 reports with itemized donor data. If so, update the top_donors entries to reflect 2026 cycle contributions and cite those specific FEC filings. If only career House data is available, ensure the clarifying note remains prominent to prevent reader misinterpretation.

reviews.summary: This profile demonstrates exemplary editorial discipline, transparency, and source rigor across the board. The researcher has meticulously documented iterations, corrected multiple factual errors (Mike Collins' education, Buddy Carter's H.R. 2 vote, misattributed donor data), and provided honest confidence disclosures where platforms are thin or absent. The issue stances across all four candidates are well-sourced, and the language is consistently neutral and fair. However, three critical factual errors must be resolved before publication: (1) Jon Ossoff's recorded 'no' vote on PN1522 (Jerome Powell Fed Chair confirmation) contradicts the 80–19 final Senate tally with near-universal Democratic support and requires immediate re-verification; (2) Mike Collins' and Derek Dooley's top_donors arrays remain empty despite documented FEC filings, creating material completeness gaps; and (3) several Ossoff foreign-policy and immigration sources lack direct press-release URLs. Additionally, Derek Dooley's platforms across reproductive rights, foreign policy, and election reform remain undefined with low-confidence ratings, reflecting genuine gaps in his public messaging. When these factual and completeness issues are addressed, this will be a model profile.

flags.concern: Jon Ossoff is recorded as voting 'no' on PN1522 (Jerome Powell Fed Chair confirmation). This contradicts historical records of the 80-19 bipartisan confirmation.
flags.suggestion: Verify against the official Senate roll-call record (Vote 176, 117th Congress). Correct to 'yes' if the record indicates confirmation support, or provide an explanatory note if the 'no' vote is accurate.

flags.concern: The top_donors array is empty for Mike Collins, despite an active Senate committee (C00544684) with accessible itemized FEC disclosures.
flags.suggestion: Access the committee's FEC reports and populate the array with the five largest contributors, citing specific FEC filing documents.

flags.concern: The top_donors array is empty for Derek Dooley despite his campaign reporting significant receipts.
flags.suggestion: Populate the array using itemized receipts from FEC committee C00914531 and cite the corresponding FEC filings.

flags.concern: A 'yes' vote on H.J.Res. 20 (CRA disapproval of a banking rule) is highly atypical for a Democrat and warrants re-verification.
flags.suggestion: Cross-reference with the Senate roll-call page (Vote 207, 119th Congress) to confirm the vote direction and bill content.

flags.concern: The polling section contains redundant entries for the same survey, which inflates the apparent volume of data.
flags.suggestion: Consolidate duplicate entries into a single, clearly labeled entry per unique survey.

flags.concern: The stance is marked 'low' confidence with no platform identified, which is a major completeness gap for a 2026 Georgia Senate candidate.
flags.suggestion: Explicitly state: 'No detailed reproductive-rights platform has been identified in reviewed campaign materials as of March 2026' to maintain transparency while keeping 'low' confidence.

reviews.summary: This candidate profile displays impressive editorial diligence, particularly in its transparent iteration notes and the clear, honest communication of low-confidence issues. The researcher has successfully resolved several previous inaccuracies. However, there are critical factual errors in legislative records that must be corrected before publication, specifically regarding Senator Ossoff’s voting history, as well as a significant completeness gap regarding campaign finance disclosures for Republican candidates.

flags.concern: Jon Ossoff's recorded 'no' vote on PN1522 (Jerome H. Powell's nomination) contradicts historical Senate records, which show a 80-19 confirmation with near-universal Democratic support, indicating a factual inaccuracy.
flags.suggestion: Verify against the official Senate roll-call record at https://www.senate.gov/legislative/LIS/roll_call_votes/vote1172/vote_117_2_00176.htm and update the vote to 'yes' if confirmed, or add an explanatory note if the 'no' vote is accurate.

flags.concern: The 'yes' vote attributed to Jon Ossoff on H.J.Res. 20 (CRA disapproval of the Bank Merger Act rule) is atypical for a Democratic senator and requires verification, as it may not align with party positions or official records.
flags.suggestion: Cross-check the Senate roll-call for Vote 207 (119th Congress, 1st Session) at https://www.senate.gov/legislative/LIS/roll_call_votes/vote1191/vote_119_1_00207.htm and correct the vote or add clarification if discrepancies are found.

flags.concern: The top_donors array is empty for Mike Collins, despite his FEC committee (C00544684) having itemized filings, creating a major completeness gap in campaign finance data.
flags.suggestion: Access Mike Collins' FEC filings for his 2026 Senate campaign and populate the array with the five largest contributors, citing specific reports (e.g., 2025 Year-End Report) for accuracy.

flags.concern: The top_donors array is empty for Derek Dooley, even though his campaign reported significant fundraising (e.g., $3 million in 2025), leaving a key aspect of his profile incomplete.
flags.suggestion: Retrieve the top five donors from Derek Dooley's FEC committee (C00914531) filings and update the array with proper citations to ensure completeness.

flags.concern: The source URL may not directly support the described investigation into human rights abuses in immigration detention, potentially affecting source quality and verifiability.
flags.suggestion: Verify that the URL resolves to the correct press release; if not, replace with a credible alternative from Ossoff's official site or a news article documenting the investigation.

flags.concern: The URL combines unrelated topics (Iran threats and Fulton County raid), which could dilute its relevance and make verification of the foreign policy stance more difficult.
flags.suggestion: Confirm the URL matches the described content; if it does not, update to a more targeted source from Ossoff's press releases or related news coverage.

flags.concern: The stance is marked with 'low' confidence and lacks detail, creating a completeness gap for a major issue in Georgia's political landscape.
flags.suggestion: Review Dooley's campaign materials and news sources for any statements; if none are found, explicitly note the absence of a platform in the stance field for transparency.

flags.concern: The stance is undefined beyond general Trump alignment, resulting in a significant completeness gap for this key Senate race issue.
flags.suggestion: Search for specific foreign policy positions in Dooley's interviews or site; if unavailable, update to clearly state the lack of articulated views while retaining 'low' confidence.

flags.concern: The stance is vague and marked with 'low' confidence, missing specifics on Georgia-specific issues like voter ID, which affects overall completeness.
flags.suggestion: Incorporate any available statements from Dooley's campaign; if none exist, add a note clarifying the absence of a detailed platform.

flags.concern: The polling section includes redundant entries from the same pollster, reducing diversity and potentially misleading users about the breadth of available data.
flags.suggestion: Consolidate duplicate entries and add a note clarifying that only one independent poll was available as of late March 2026, or include any new polls if they exist.

flags.concern: The race description lacks precise runoff date details, making it less complete for users needing a full election timeline.
flags.suggestion: Update to specify the exact runoff date (e.g., July 28, 2026) based on official Georgia election schedules for improved accuracy and utility.

reviews.summary: This profile demonstrates thorough research and transparency in iteration notes, with accurate corrections to candidate backgrounds and strong official sourcing for most issues. However, it includes critical factual errors in Jon Ossoff's voting records, significant gaps in top donors for Mike Collins and Derek Dooley, and unverified or incomplete source URLs that undermine overall reliability and completeness.

polling_note: As of late March 2026, only one independent public poll of this race has been published. Additional surveys are expected as the May 2026 primary approaches.
